mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week as recession fears dominate pricing.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based demand growth, driving positive equity returns over the next month through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals from RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day returns in over 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production indicates capacity utilization stress, which suppresses corporate profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases (out-of-sample accuracy < 45%).

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising nonfarm payrolls create a liquidity-fueled growth environment that forces equity prices higher over the next month as risk appetite expands.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered cases, breaking the liquidity-growth transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 2 percent signals immediate consumer withdrawal, causing rapid negative S&P 500 repricing within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances following a >2% RSAFS decline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial output alongside falling unemployment indicates productive capacity expansion, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns during early-cycle phases with a Rank IC below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings force expectations of tighter monetary policy, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases show no statistically significant negative correlation with 20-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10).
